what_it_does: A catalog/ledger of design pieces (mockups, screens, flows, states, components, live compositions, and corpus studies) for the Counterpart design system, recording per-piece metadata, versioning, taxonomy, and the language concepts each piece demonstrates.
observations: Lists 60+ mockup entries across slides, application UI, live-rendered surfaces, corpus pieces, and decision surfaces.
Each mockup carries dials (warmth numeric + register categorical), a human note, and often a developer note.
Versions are tracked per mockup with v number, file path, date, user-facing changes, dev-facing changes_dev, and a canonical flag.
Top-level groups array organizes pieces; categories array maps those groups into 'slides', 'application', and 'further out'.
kinds array enumerates kind values and the demonstrated concepts associated with each kind.
Several entries are marked as live or data-driven (renderUnit, renderPiece, renderArchetype, story-tree).
Decision-surface entries reference real pending company decisions (rerank-loadout, form-taxonomy, event-streams, etc.) rendered from authored records.
Superseded_by links point from older explorations to newer canonical pieces.
Canvas dimensions appear only on some entries, mostly full-size phone screens.
Future pending categories (Scenes, Documents, Social tiles, Email & letters) are declared with no pieces yet.
standouts: The manifest treats design artifacts as versioned, typed substrates with explicit intent, dials, and demonstrated concepts.
Decision-surface pieces are wired to real company decisions and rendered through a generic archetype renderer.
Live-composed pieces are explicitly distinguished from frozen mockups, showing a move toward data-driven design surfaces.
Categories include both existing groups and a 'further out' bucket for unbuilt artifact types.
conventions: Consistent per-mockup schema: id, title, group, dials, note, versions, kind, demonstrates.
Dual change logs: 'changes' for design intent, 'changes_dev' for implementation specifics.
File paths follow pieces/<id>/vN.html or pieces/<thematic-path>/<file>.html.
Group names use the '·' separator and title-case phrases.
Dials pair a numeric warmth value with a categorical register.
Supersession is expressed via a superseded_by id reference.
Canonical flags live inside version objects rather than on the mockup root.
concerns: Canvas dimensions are present on some mockups but absent on others, even within the same kind.
The top-level groups list includes 'Grown on the bench', which no mockup currently uses.
The 'instrument' kind lists demonstrates values (graph, wayfinder) but no mockups of that kind exist.
Some superseded entries retain detailed flaw notes, which may confuse consumers expecting only current work.
Date strings are all in 2026, which is file-local but worth noting as future-dated.
The distinction between 'Application · screens' and 'Application · screens, full size' is not explicitly explained in the schema.
notes: This file is the master index of the Counterpart design workshop: every piece, its intent, version history, demonstrated concept, and its place in the slide/app/live taxonomy.
questions: What is the exact scale and intended mapping of the warmth dial?
How are demonstrates values validated against the kinds list?
Does 'destined': 'live-surface' indicate a runtime target, a rendering mode, or a review destination?
What process updates canonical flags and superseded_by links?
Are groups authoritative from the top-level list or derived from categories?
purpose_vs_actual: The file has no explicit stated purpose in its content, but it functions as a versioned design ledger and taxonomy; that matches what it actually contains.
apparent_themes: Design-as-code: mockups are versioned, inspectable artifacts.
Generative surfaces: separation of data records from rendering archetypes.
Expressive dials: warmth and register as first-class metadata.
Explicit evolution: versioning and supersession are part of the schema.
Taxonomy of demonstration: every piece is tied to a language concept it proves.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to make the design system machine-readable, navigable, and decision-aware, so that each piece can be composed, superseded, and traced to the language concept it validates.
novelty: It treats design mockups as a ledger with version history, canonical flags, supersession, live-vs-frozen classification, and binds real pending company decisions to a generic renderer.
contribution: A structured, queryable index that preserves the intent, iteration, and relationships of each design piece, enabling navigation from concept to artifact and from older takes to current takes.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON manifest cataloging Counterpart design pieces with metadata including group, dials, versions, canonical flags, demonstrated concepts, supersession, and taxonomy into categories and kinds.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a machine-readable ledger of design substrates so the system can navigate, compose, and reason about pieces, their evolution, and the language concepts they demonstrate.
